natural_language: A retailer decides a single-period order quantity for one product. Demand is 71. Unit ordering cost is 2, holding cost for leftover inventory is 4, and shortage penalty is 15. Formulate and solve a linear optimization model.

A firm plans replenishment for one item over 3 periods. Initial inventory is 2. Period demands are [49, 21, 38]. Unit ordering cost is 5 and holding cost is 1. Use inventory balance constraints to minimize total ordering and holding cost.

A firm plans replenishment for one item over 4 periods with backorders allowed. Initial inventory is 1. Period demands are [25, 47, 46, 24]. Unit ordering cost is 2, holding cost is 1, and shortage penalty is 16. Formulate a linear model with inventory and shortage variables.

A warehouse replenishes 2 items over 4 periods. Initial inventories are [7, 1]. Demand matrix by item and period is [[46, 17, 24, 50], [50, 47, 13, 46]]. Item volumes are [4, 1] and storage capacity per period is 151. Minimize ordering and holding costs subject to inventory balance and capacity constraints.

A firm plans replenishment over 3 periods with fixed ordering cost. Initial inventory is 5. Demands are [51, 34, 50]. Unit ordering cost is 1, holding cost is 2, fixed ordering cost is 43. Use binary order trigger variables and Big-M constraints with M = 135.
